game_name: Desert Strike: Return to the Gulf

danger_signals.visual_form: Grounded enemy planes, AA missile launchers, radar installations visible on terrain
danger_signals.meaning: Active military threat requiring destruction; areas of concentrated danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Ground-based armor units rendered in isometric view
danger_signals.meaning: Heavy defensive positions; localized danger zones

danger_signals.visual_form: Chemical weapons delivery systems visible on map
danger_signals.meaning: Strategic threat; mission-critical targets indicating escalating danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Industrial/military compound structures marked on terrain
danger_signals.meaning: Existential threat level; highest priority danger zones

danger_signals.visual_form: Power plant installations, weapons assembly structures
danger_signals.meaning: Apocalyptic threat; final mission danger indicator

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual projectile paths from enemy positions
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate spatial danger; requires evasive maneuvering

safety_signals.visual_form: Designated extraction points visible on terrain
safety_signals.meaning: Safe completion areas; hostage/prisoner delivery destinations
Resource availability; survival mechanic; navigation waypoints

safety_signals.visual_form: Ammo supply containers visible in environment

safety_signals.meaning: Mission objectives providing armor restoration when airlifted

safety_signals.signal: Open terrain with minimal installation density
safety_signals.visual_form: Empty desert areas visible in isometric scrolling

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Apache helicopter (AH-64 variant with Fenestron rotor)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Landing zones; pro-American installations; embassy positions

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Enemy military installations; dictatorial compound (private yacht)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Soldiers with small arms; anti-aircraft batteries; tanks; armored vehicles; scud launchers
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Airstrips and support facilities; chemical weapons plants; biological weapons plants; nuclear facilities; military fortifications; private yacht (final stronghold)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Hostages; POWs; U.N. inspection teams; embassy personnel; local civilians
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Marked rescue locations; embassy compound; detention areas

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy airstrips and support facilities visible; concentration of aircraft and air defense systems on terrain
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy air defenses activate when player approaches; aircraft remain grounded until destroyed

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Chemical weapons plant and scud launcher batteries rendered as major industrial/military installations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Chemical-armed missiles pose heightened danger; multiple targets require sequential destruction; civilian evacuation points emerge

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: American embassy compound visible on map; increased hostage positions; yacht becomes visible as new target location
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Kilbaba location shifts to yacht; enemy defenses concentrate around embassy and yacht; hostage rescue activates companion rescue mechanic

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Nuclear power plant; nuclear weapons assembly components; garbage trucks carrying nuclear material visible on terrain; oil production facility under threat
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Kilbaba utilizes all remaining defenses; nuclear materials in civilian transport indicate desperation; final confrontation imminent

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Armor depletion visual indicator visible on player HUD/helicopter state
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Helicopter becomes more vulnerable; increased enemy aggression; armor restoration through rescues becomes critical

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Fuel gauge depletes visually; strategic fuel barrel placement becomes critical visual pathway
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must plan mission routes around fuel locations; crash-on-empty mechanic forces navigation discipline

environmental_storytelling.example: Early missions show scattered airstrips; later missions show concentrated nuclear facilities indicating Kilbaba's escalating weapons development
Kilbaba moves from fortified ground position to mobile yacht location, showing desperation and flee strategy
Chemical then biological then nuclear weapons appear sequentially in missions, showing escalating threat level and regime desperation
Concentration of hostages, POWs, and embassy personnel visually indicates civil infrastructure under hostile control
Fuel and ammunition scarcity forces player to choose efficient routes and weapon loadouts, mirroring military strategic planning
Multi-directional isometric scrolling reveals Persian Gulf emirate territory under occupation; open desert emphasizes scale of conflict

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Fire projectiles at approaching helicopter; require strategic approach or destruction
Attack when player enters proximity; concentrate around high-value targets
Remain on airstrips; do not engage until destroyed or player approaches
Pose chemical weapons threat; higher priority targeting than standard installations
Remain in place until airlifted; provide armor restoration when transported to landing zones
Flees from yacht when player approaches; relocates to final stronghold; activates all remaining defenses in final mission
Fuel depletes steadily; armor depletes on enemy contact; must navigate between fuel/ammo sources; weapon selection varies by situation

notes: Desert Strike uses isometric perspective and multi-directional scrolling to create strategic, non-linear mission spaces. Danger and safety signals are environmental and positional rather than UI-driven (typical for early 1990s action games). The game's political narrative — U.S. military intervention against a Gulf dictator developing WMDs — is communicated through mission objectives and target hierarchy rather than explicit faction visual branding. World state progresses through clear mission phases with escalating weapon threats (air→chemical→biological→nuclear), creating narrative tension through target type. Resource management (fuel, ammo, armor) serves dual function: gameplay mechanic and strategic simulation of military logistics. The helicopter's external third-person camera (rather than cockpit view) emphasizes tactical overview and environmental scanning.
